title: FakeMultica response/record v1
description: Explicit fixture for the fake multica CLI: a response (stdout/stderr/exit_code) and a record (argv/cwd/env/timestamp). stdout and stderr are base64-encoded UTF-8 bytes to make binary-safe fixtures explicit. Top-level schema version pins the contract to v1.

stdout_b64.description: Base64-encoded UTF-8 bytes written to the child's stdout.

stderr_b64.description: Base64-encoded UTF-8 bytes written to the child's stderr.

exit_code.description: Process exit code. 0 means success; 64 (EX_USAGE) means a missing/corrupt record path.

argv.description: Absolute argv the child was invoked with (argv[0] is the script path, not the package name).

cwd.description: Absolute working directory the child observed at invocation.

env.description: Allowlisted env vars captured at invocation. Values are redacted where required.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the invocation.
